content:
# Adsorption

**Summary**: The physical or chemical bonding of molecules (solutes) to a surface.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] studied the adsorption of heavy metals onto MWCNTs.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] evaluated the role of adsorption by MWCNTs in the treatment process.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing MTBE from water using fly ash.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] investigated adsorption for heavy metal removal from water.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing sulfur compounds from fuel.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] evaluated the removal of Hg2+ using batch-mode adsorption with AgNP, Fe2O3, and NZVI.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] studied adsorption mechanisms for removing Cd(II) ions from water.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] investigated the adsorption of MTBE onto various types of SDAC.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] evaluated the removal efficiency of phenol and cadmium via adsorption onto modified graphene.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing methylene blue and heavy metals from wastewater.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] investigated adsorption of arsenic onto biochar surfaces.

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] studied the adsorption of MTBE and BTEX onto CAC.

## Scientific Context

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] Adsorption is one of the most promising methods for removing heavy metals from water.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] evaluated the role of adsorption by MWCNTs in the treatment process.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing MTBE from water using fly ash.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] investigated adsorption for heavy metal removal from water.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing sulfur compounds from fuel.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] evaluated the removal of Hg2+ using batch-mode adsorption with AgNP, Fe2O3, and NZVI.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] studied adsorption mechanisms for removing Cd(II) ions from water.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] investigated the adsorption of MTBE onto various types of SDAC.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] evaluated the removal efficiency of phenol and cadmium via adsorption onto modified graphene.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] studied adsorption as the primary mechanism for removing methylene blue and heavy metals from wastewater.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] investigated adsorption of arsenic onto biochar surfaces.

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] studied the adsorption of MTBE and BTEX onto CAC.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] Batch mode adsorption experiments.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- MWCNTs are good adsorbents for removing Hg(II) and As(III) due to high surface area. ([[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009, p. 11]])

- By-products were below detectable levels when adsorption was the primary mechanism rather than photocatalysis. ([[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013, p. 54]])

- Silver oxide impregnated-FA showed significant adsorption (24% in English summary, 42% in Arabic) compared to other materials. ([[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014, p. 12]])

- The experimental data fitted Langmuir, Freundlich, and Temkin isotherms to describe the adsorption process. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 13]])

- Adsorption was evaluated using various models including Langmuir and Freundlich isotherms. ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 117]])

- Adsorption efficiency was found to be dependent on pH, contact time, and adsorbent dosage. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 21]])

- Adsorption was found to be highly dependent on pH, with optimal removal at pH 9. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 14]])

- Adsorbent 2Z-700-90 achieved a removal efficiency of 70% under optimized conditions. ([[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018, p. 12]])

- Removal efficiency was influenced by pH, agitation speed, temperature, dosage, concentration, and time. ([[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019, p. 42]])

- PG showed high capacity (161.29 mg/g) for methylene blue removal. ([[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021, p. 19]])

- Adsorption is highly dependent on several factors including pH, presence of other cations, and adsorbent dosage; it can achieve high removal rates with easy operational controls. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 26]])

- MTBE and BTEX adsorption is mostly affected by CAC dosage, initial contaminant concentrations, and flowrate. ([[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025, p. 13]])
